About.title: What is this website for?
About.description: The <aip>Norwegian Aeronautical Information Publication AIP</aip> is available as a free version from Avinor. This site aims to make it easier to find airfields, as the basic version does not include a search function. We do not accept any liability for the accuracy or timeliness of the AIP (Aeronautical Information Publication), as it is not operated by us. We only link to the respective approach charts.

Common.homeLink: AIP:Aero - Aeronautical Information Publications (AIP) & approach charts for Europe
Common.installHint: Install as an app: open your browser's Share menu and choose 'Add to Home Screen'. Then open the app once while online and save your fields there again - the app has its own offline storage.
Common.installHintMac: On a Mac: install via Safari's Share menu using 'Add to Dock'. Then open the app once while online and save your fields there again - the app has its own offline storage.
Common.bulkNoSpace: Not enough free storage on this device
Common.bulkScope: Included: all airfield pages with aerodrome data. Not included: approach chart PDFs (can be saved per airfield) and live weather (needs a connection).

TermsPage.title: Terms of Service
TermsPage.intro: AIP:Aero is a free search and index service for Aeronautical Information Publications (AIP), approach charts and airport data across Europe.
TermsPage.servicesText: AIP:Aero lets you find aerodromes by name or ICAO code and takes you directly to the official AIP publication of the respective country. In addition, our airport pages embed supplementary information such as weather reports (METAR/TAF), aerodrome data (runways, frequencies, elevation), maps and contact details, and pages can be saved for offline use. The service is provided free of charge and without registration.
TermsPage.disclaimerText: AIP:Aero cannot be held liable under any circumstances. We do not publish aeronautical documents ourselves: we only link to the latest Aeronautical Information Publication (AIP) made available by the official provider of the respective country. The same applies to weather data (METAR/TAF) and airport data: we embed this information on our pages, but it originates from the external sources listed below. We assume no liability or warranty for the accuracy, completeness or timeliness of any linked or embedded content, nor for the availability of this website. This website does not replace the official pre-flight briefing: the pilot in command remains solely responsible for obtaining and verifying all information required for a flight.
TermsPage.sourcesIntro: We obtain the linked and embedded data from the following sources (addresses shown as plain text, deliberately not clickable):
TermsPage.sourcesAip: Official AIP publications we link to
TermsPage.srcOpenAip: extended aerodrome data: runways, frequencies, fuel
TermsPage.metaDescription: Terms of service of AIP:Aero, the free AIP and approach chart search for Norway and Europe: service description, disclaimer of liability and data sources.
